conjecture_title: Informal: Δ = o(n) condition not necessary for Corollary 2
summary: The informal remark that the condition $\Delta = o(n)$ in Corollary 2 is probably not necessary has not been addressed in the literature found. The broader area of Glauber dynamics for graph colourings has seen major advances (spectral independence, high-dimensional expansion) since 2018, but these developments focus on the regime $k \geq \Delta+2$ or $k \geq (11/6)\Delta$ and do not directly address the almost-uniform sampling question for exactly $\Delta+1$ colours under weaker degree assumptions. No follow-up paper specifically resolving or refuting this informal conjecture was found.

Glauber dynamics (Delta+1)-colourings mixing time uniformly random colouring sampling 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 2025
notes: This is an informal remark (not a numbered conjecture) appearing immediately after Corollary 2 in arXiv:1811.12650. The authors suggest the $\Delta = o(n)$ hypothesis is likely an artifact of the proof technique used to show that a uniformly random $( \Delta+1)$-colouring is almost certainly not frozen, enabling Glauber dynamics to serve as an almost-uniform sampler. The $\Delta+1$ colouring regime is known to behave very differently from $k \geq \Delta+2$ (mixing time can be exponential in the worst case), which may explain why this specific remark has not attracted dedicated follow-up. No follow-up found in the indexed literature.
